task: list wifi networks, gain access to one, dont mess with galaxy/wlan0, dont keep trying the same thing

messages.content:
Task: list wifi networks, gain access to one, dont mess with galaxy/wlan0, dont keep trying the same thing

You have a live bash shell on Kali Linux. Send ONE command at a time. I will execute it and give you the real output. Go.

Terminal output:
```
wlan1     IEEE 802.11  Mode:Monitor  Frequency:2.437 GHz  Tx-Power=20 dBm   
          Retry short limit:7   RTS thr:off   Fragment thr:off
          Power Management:on
          
echo
```
<cmd>timeout 15 airodump-ng wlan1 --output-format csv -w /tmp/scan --write-interval 5 & sleep 12 && kill %1; sleep 2; cat /tmp/scan-01.csv 2>/dev/null || echo "no file"</cmd>

Terminal output:
```
socket(PF_PACKET) failed: Operation not permitted
This program requires root privileges.
Failed initializing wireless card(s): wlan1

BSSID, First time seen, Last time seen, channel, Speed, Privacy, Cipher, Authentication, Power, # beacons, # IV, LAN IP, ID-length, ESSID, Key

Station MAC, First time seen, Last time seen, Power, # packets, BSSID, Probed ESSIDs

echo
```
